text:
You have seen a
above that if the three angles of one triangle
are respectively
equal to the three angles of another triangle, then their
corresponding
sides are
proportional (i.e., in the same ratio). What about the converse of this statement? Is the
converse true? In other words, if the sides of a triangle are respectively p
proportional to
the sides of another triangle, is it true that their corresponding angles are equal? Let us
examine it through an activity :
